name: Vector Algebra
subject: Maths

sections.heading: 10.1 Scalars vs Vectors
sections.body:
A scalar has only magnitude (mass, time, temperature). A vector has both magnitude and direction (displacement, velocity, force).

Vectors are represented by directed line segments. Two vectors are equal if they have the same magnitude and direction (no fixed location).

The position vector of a point $P(x,y,z)$ relative to the origin is $\vec{OP}=x\hat\imath+y\hat\jmath+z\hat k.$

Magnitude: $|\vec a|=\sqrt{a_1^{2}+a_2^{2}+a_3^{2}}$ for $\vec a=a_1\hat\imath+a_2\hat\jmath+a_3\hat k$.

sections.heading: 10.2 Types of Vectors & Direction Cosines
sections.body:
• Zero vector $\vec 0$: zero magnitude; direction undefined.
  • Unit vector: magnitude $=1$. The unit vector along $\vec a$ is $\hat a=\dfrac{\vec a}{|\vec a|}$.
  • Coinitial: same starting point. Collinear: parallel (same or opposite direction). Equal: same magnitude AND direction.
  • Negative $-\vec a$: same magnitude, opposite direction.

Direction cosines. If $\vec a$ makes angles $\alpha,\beta,\gamma$ with the $x,y,z$ axes, then $l=\cos\alpha,\;m=\cos\beta,\;n=\cos\gamma$ and $l^{2}+m^{2}+n^{2}=1.$

Direction ratios: any triple $a:b:c$ proportional to $l:m:n$. From $\vec a=a_1\hat\imath+a_2\hat\jmath+a_3\hat k$: direction cosines $=\dfrac{a_1}{|\vec a|},\dfrac{a_2}{|\vec a|},\dfrac{a_3}{|\vec a|}$.

sections.heading: 10.3 Addition, Subtraction, Scalar Multiplication
sections.body:
Triangle law: if $\vec a$ and $\vec b$ are placed head-to-tail, then $\vec a+\vec b$ goes from the tail of $\vec a$ to the head of $\vec b$.

Parallelogram law: if $\vec a,\vec b$ are coinitial, $\vec a+\vec b$ is the diagonal of the parallelogram from that common point.

Component-wise: $(a_1\hat\imath+a_2\hat\jmath+a_3\hat k)+(b_1\hat\imath+b_2\hat\jmath+b_3\hat k)=(a_1+b_1)\hat\imath+\dots$

Scalar multiplication: $\lambda\vec a$ has magnitude $|\lambda||\vec a|$ and direction same as (resp. opposite to) $\vec a$ when $\lambda>0$ (resp. $\lambda<0$).

Section formula. The point dividing $\vec a,\vec b$ in ratio $m:n$ internally is $\dfrac{n\vec a+m\vec b}{m+n}$; externally, $\dfrac{m\vec b-n\vec a}{m-n}$.

sections.heading: 10.4 Dot (Scalar) Product
sections.body:
$\vec a\cdot\vec b=|\vec a||\vec b|\cos\theta$ where $\theta\in[0,\pi]$ is the angle between them.

Component form: $\vec a\cdot\vec b=a_1 b_1+a_2 b_2+a_3 b_3.$

Properties: commutative ($\vec a\cdot\vec b=\vec b\cdot\vec a$); distributive over addition; $\vec a\cdot\vec a=|\vec a|^{2}$; $\vec a\perp\vec b\Leftrightarrow\vec a\cdot\vec b=0$.

Projection. Scalar projection of $\vec a$ on $\vec b$ is $\dfrac{\vec a\cdot\vec b}{|\vec b|}$; the vector projection is $\dfrac{\vec a\cdot\vec b}{|\vec b|^{2}}\vec b$.

sections.heading: 10.5 Cross (Vector) Product
sections.body:
$\vec a\times\vec b=|\vec a||\vec b|\sin\theta\,\hat n$, where $\hat n$ is a unit vector perpendicular to both $\vec a$ and $\vec b$ in the right-hand-rule direction.

Determinant form: $\vec a\times\vec b=\begin{vmatrix}\hat\imath&\hat\jmath&\hat k\\a_1&a_2&a_3\\b_1&b_2&b_3\end{vmatrix}.$

Properties: not commutative: $\vec a\times\vec b=-\vec b\times\vec a$. Distributive. $\vec a\times\vec a=\vec 0$. $\vec a\parallel\vec b\Leftrightarrow\vec a\times\vec b=\vec 0$.

Geometric interpretations:

  • Area of parallelogram with adjacent sides $\vec a,\vec b$: $|\vec a\times\vec b|$.
  • Area of triangle with sides $\vec a,\vec b$: $\dfrac{1}{2}|\vec a\times\vec b|$.
  • $\sin\theta=\dfrac{|\vec a\times\vec b|}{|\vec a||\vec b|}$.

sections.heading: 10.6 Standard Identities and Examples
sections.body:
• $\hat\imath\cdot\hat\imath=\hat\jmath\cdot\hat\jmath=\hat k\cdot\hat k=1$; $\hat\imath\cdot\hat\jmath=\hat\jmath\cdot\hat k=\hat k\cdot\hat\imath=0$.
  • $\hat\imath\times\hat\jmath=\hat k$, $\hat\jmath\times\hat k=\hat\imath$, $\hat k\times\hat\imath=\hat\jmath$ (cyclic).
  • Lagrange identity: $|\vec a\times\vec b|^{2}+(\vec a\cdot\vec b)^{2}=|\vec a|^{2}|\vec b|^{2}$.

summary: Vectors carry magnitude and direction. Express via components $\vec a=a_1\hat\imath+a_2\hat\jmath+a_3\hat k$. Direction cosines satisfy $l^{2}+m^{2}+n^{2}=1$. Dot product $\vec a\cdot\vec b=|\vec a||\vec b|\cos\theta$ measures projection and tests perpendicularity; cross product $\vec a\times\vec b=|\vec a||\vec b|\sin\theta\,\hat n$ produces a perpendicular vector whose magnitude equals the parallelogram area. The cross product is anti-commutative; the dot product is commutative. Triangle area $=\tfrac{1}{2}|\vec a\times\vec b|$.
pitfalls: Confusing the sign in $\vec a\times\vec b=-\vec b\times\vec a$.
Forgetting that $\hat n$ in the cross product follows the right-hand rule.
Computing $|\vec a||\vec b|\cos\theta$ but using $\theta$ outside $[0,\pi]$.
Treating $\vec a\cdot\vec b=0$ as $\vec a=\vec 0$ or $\vec b=\vec 0$ — actually means perpendicular.
Using the section formula with the wrong $m,n$ assignment for internal vs external division.
